texto: Almsgiving is a major tenet of Islam. It's also a kick in the pants of the highest order. Nothing is as cool as skimming off a couple of hundred thousand from some multinational corporation and handing it out to random strangers. Or writing a harmless little virus that makes credit counters "forget" to send a surcharge back to the bank after each purchase. Oh, sure, technically I'm supposed to give away my own money, but whatever. I'm sure Allah gets the spirit of what I'm doing here.
explicacao: Citação de Lyda Morehouse sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Almsgiving is a major tenet of Islam. It's also a kick in the pants of the highest order. Nothing…” articula reflexao, science-fiction, almsgiving em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e reflexao.

frase_original: Almsgiving is a major tenet of Islam. It's also a kick in the pants of the highest order. Nothing is as cool as skimming off a couple of hundred thousand from some multinational corporation and handing it out to random strangers. Or writing a harmless little virus that makes credit counters "forget" to send a surcharge back to the bank after each purchase. Oh, sure, technically I'm supposed to give away my own money, but whatever. I'm sure Allah gets the spirit of what I'm doing here.

texto: I never sleep. Like the dolphin and the spiny anteater, I don't experience REM. Unlike the dreamless mammals, I'm a construct. I am a living program inside a vast network of electronic impulses known as the LINK. In that datastream I've uncovered the meaning of another kind of dreaming--that of a fond hope or aspiration, a yearning, a desire, or a passion. This much I have. When I dream, I dream of Mecca.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Lyda Morehouse: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I never sleep. Like the dolphin and the spiny anteater, I don't experience REM. Unlike the dreaml…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, first-lines, science-fiction pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e science-fiction.

frase_original: I never sleep. Like the dolphin and the spiny anteater, I don't experience REM. Unlike the dreamless mammals, I'm a construct. I am a living program inside a vast network of electronic impulses known as the LINK. In that datastream I've uncovered the meaning of another kind of dreaming--that of a fond hope or aspiration, a yearning, a desire, or a passion. This much I have. When I dream, I dream of Mecca.

texto: Where was this written? I wondered. Where in the Talmud or the Koran or the Bible did it say, "Lo, and Satan fetcheth the coffee for the Antichrist and her minions"?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Lyda Morehouse que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “Where was this written? I wondered. Where in the Talmud or the Koran or the Bible did it say, "Lo…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, science-fiction, where, com foco em reflexao.
